info.description: Defold's built-in message ids and the payload shape each one carries.

messages.name: acquire_input_focus
messages.description: Acquire input focus for the current game object.

messages.name: release_input_focus
messages.description: Release input focus held by the current game object.

messages.name: enable
messages.description: Enable the receiving component.

messages.name: disable
messages.description: Disable the receiving component.

messages.name: set_parent
messages.description: Re-parent the receiving game object.

payload.name: parent_id
payload.doc: Hash of the new parent (omit or nil to clear).

payload.name: keep_world_transform
payload.doc: 1 to preserve world transform; 0 (default) to keep local transform.

messages.name: play_animation
messages.description: Play a flipbook/model animation.

payload.name: id
payload.doc: Hashed animation id.

messages.name: animation_done
messages.description: Sent back when a one-shot animation finishes.

payload.name: current_tile
payload.doc: Tile index when the animation ended.

payload.name: id
payload.doc: Hashed animation id that finished.

messages.name: collision_response
messages.description: Reported once per collision pair per frame.

payload.name: other_id
payload.doc: Id of the other colliding object.

payload.name: other_position
payload.doc: World position of the other object.

payload.name: other_group
payload.doc: Collision group of the other object.

payload.name: own_group
payload.doc: Collision group of this object.

messages.name: trigger_response
messages.description: Reported when a trigger overlap begins or ends.

payload.name: other_id
payload.doc: Id of the object entering/leaving the trigger.

payload.name: enter
payload.doc: true on enter, false on exit.

payload.name: other_group
payload.doc: Collision group of the other object.

payload.name: own_group
payload.doc: Collision group of this object.

messages.name: contact_point_response
messages.description: Reported once per contact point per frame.

payload.name: position
payload.doc: Contact point position.

payload.name: normal
payload.doc: Contact normal.

payload.name: relative_velocity
payload.doc: Relative velocity at the contact point.

payload.name: distance
payload.doc: Penetration distance.

payload.name: applied_impulse
payload.doc: Impulse applied to resolve the contact.

payload.name: life_time
payload.doc: Contact life time.

payload.name: mass
payload.doc: Mass of this object.

payload.name: other_mass
payload.doc: Mass of the other object.

payload.name: other_id
payload.doc: Id of the other object.

payload.name: other_position
payload.doc: World position of the other object.

payload.name: other_group
payload.doc: Collision group of the other object.

payload.name: own_group
payload.doc: Collision group of this object.

messages.name: apply_force
messages.description: Apply a force at a world position on a collision object.

payload.name: force
payload.doc: Force vector in Newtons.

payload.name: position
payload.doc: World position at which the force is applied.

messages.name: ray_cast_response
messages.description: Reported when an asynchronous ray cast hits a collision object.

payload.name: fraction
payload.doc: Fraction of the ray length at which the hit occurred (0..1).

payload.name: position
payload.doc: World position of the hit.

payload.name: normal
payload.doc: World-space surface normal at the hit.

payload.name: id
payload.doc: Id of the hit collision object.

payload.name: group
payload.doc: Collision group of the hit object.

payload.name: request_id
payload.doc: Request id passed to physics.raycast_async.

messages.name: ray_cast_missed
messages.description: Reported when an asynchronous ray cast hits nothing.

payload.name: group
payload.doc: Collision group mask used for the ray cast.

payload.name: request_id
payload.doc: Request id passed to physics.raycast_async.

messages.name: proxy_loaded
messages.description: Sent by a collection proxy once its collection finished loading.

messages.name: load
messages.description: Tell a collection proxy to start loading its collection.

messages.name: unload
messages.description: Tell a collection proxy to unload its collection.

messages.name: init
messages.description: Initialize the loaded collection inside a collection proxy.

messages.name: final
messages.description: Finalize the loaded collection inside a collection proxy.

messages.name: play_sound
messages.description: Play the sound attached to the receiving sound component.

payload.name: delay
payload.doc: Delay in seconds before the sound starts (default 0).

payload.name: gain
payload.doc: Sound gain between 0 and 1 (default 1).

payload.name: play_id
payload.doc: Caller-chosen id echoed back in sound_done / sound_stopped.

payload.name: start_time
payload.doc: Optional start offset (seconds). Mutually exclusive with start_frame.

payload.name: start_frame
payload.doc: Optional start offset (frames). If both are provided, start_frame is used.

messages.name: stop_sound
messages.description: Stop all instances playing on the receiving sound component.

messages.name: set_gain
messages.description: Set the gain on the receiving sound component.

payload.name: gain
payload.doc: Target gain between 0 and 1.

messages.name: sound_done
messages.description: Sent back when a sound started with play_sound finishes playing.

payload.name: play_id
payload.doc: The id passed to play_sound.

messages.name: sound_stopped
messages.description: Sent back when a sound started with play_sound is stopped via stop_sound.

payload.name: play_id
payload.doc: The id passed to play_sound.

messages.name: model_animation_done
messages.description: Sent back when a play-once animation started with model.play_anim finishes.

payload.name: animation_id
payload.doc: Hashed id of the completed animation.

payload.name: playback
payload.doc: Playback mode constant of the completed animation.

messages.name: set_time_step
messages.description: Set the time-step scaling and mode for the collection inside a collection proxy.

payload.name: factor
payload.doc: Time-step scaling factor.

payload.name: mode
payload.doc: Time-step mode: 0 for continuous, 1 for discrete.

messages.name: async_load
messages.description: Tell a collection proxy to start loading its collection in the background.

messages.name: proxy_unloaded
messages.description: Sent back to the initiating script once a collection proxy finished unloading its collection.

messages.name: clear_color
messages.description: Set the colour the render target is cleared to.

payload.name: color
payload.doc: Clear colour as an RGBA vector4.

messages.name: set_view_projection
messages.description: Posted automatically by camera components to the render script with the active view and projection matrices.

payload.name: id
payload.doc: Id of the camera component that posted the matrices.

payload.name: view
payload.doc: View matrix of the camera.

payload.name: projection
payload.doc: Projection matrix of the camera.

messages.name: draw_line
messages.description: Request a debug line to be drawn for the current frame.

payload.name: start_point
payload.doc: World-space start point of the line.

payload.name: end_point
payload.doc: World-space end point of the line.

payload.name: color
payload.doc: Line colour as an RGBA vector4.

messages.name: draw_debug_text
messages.description: Request debug text to be drawn for the current frame.

payload.name: position
payload.doc: World-space position to draw the text at.

payload.name: text
payload.doc: The text to draw.

payload.name: color
payload.doc: Text colour as an RGBA vector4.

messages.name: window_resized
messages.description: Received by the render script and listeners when the application window changes size.

payload.name: height
payload.doc: New window height in pixels.

payload.name: width
payload.doc: New window width in pixels.

messages.name: layout_changed
messages.description: Sent to a gui script when the layout changes, for example when the orientation changes or the device profile is switched.

payload.name: id
payload.doc: The id of the layout the gui switched to.

payload.name: previous_id
payload.doc: The id of the layout the gui switched from.

messages.name: set_camera
messages.description: Post this message to a camera-component to set its properties at run-time.

payload.name: aspect_ratio
payload.doc: aspect ratio of the screen (width divided by height)

payload.name: fov
payload.doc: field of view of the lens, measured as the angle in radians between the right and left edge

payload.name: near_z
payload.doc: position of the near clipping plane (distance from camera along relative z)

payload.name: far_z
payload.doc: position of the far clipping plane (distance from camera along relative z)

payload.name: orthographic_projection
payload.doc: set to use an orthographic projection

payload.name: orthographic_zoom
payload.doc: positive zoom multiplier when the camera is using an orthographic projection

payload.name: orthographic_mode
payload.doc: orthographic zoom behavior when orthographic_projection is enabled

messages.name: acquire_camera_focus
messages.description: Post this message to a camera-component to activate it. Several cameras can be active at the same time, but only the camera that was last activated will be used for rendering. When the camera is deactivated (see release_camera_focus), the previously activated camera will again be used for rendering automatically. The reason it is called "camera focus" is the similarity to how acquiring input focus works (see acquire_input_focus).

messages.name: release_camera_focus
messages.description: Post this message to a camera-component to deactivate it. The camera is then removed from the active cameras. See acquire_camera_focus for more information how the active cameras are used in rendering.

messages.name: contact_point_event
messages.description: See physics.set_event_listener. This message is sent to a function specified in physics.set_event_listener when a collision object has contact points with another collision object. Since multiple contact points can occur for two colliding objects, this event can be sent multiple times in the same frame for the same two colliding objects. To only be notified once when the collision occurs, check for the collision_event event instead.

payload.name: applied_impulse
payload.doc: the impulse the contact resulted in

payload.name: distance
payload.doc: the penetration distance between the objects, which is always positive

payload.name: a
payload.doc:
contact point information for object A
position
vector3 The world position of object A
id
hash The ID of object A
group
hash The collision group of object A
relative_velocity
vector3 The relative velocity of the collision object A as observed from B object
mass
number The mass of the collision object A in kg
normal
vector3 normal in world space of the contact point, which points from B object towards A object

payload.name: b
payload.doc:
contact point information for object B
position
vector3 The world position of object B
id
hash The ID of object B
group
hash The collision group of object B
relative_velocity
vector3 The relative velocity of the collision object B as observed from A object
mass
number The mass of the collision object B in kg
normal
vector3 normal in world space of the contact point, which points from A object towards B object

messages.name: collision_event
messages.description: See physics.set_event_listener. This message is sent to a function specified in physics.set_event_listener when two collision objects collide. This message only reports that a collision has occurred and will be sent once per frame and per colliding pair. For more detailed information, check for the contact_point_event.

payload.name: a
payload.doc:
collision information for object A
position
vector3 The world position of object A
id
hash The ID of object A
group
hash The collision group of object A

payload.name: b
payload.doc:
collision information for object B
position
vector3 The world position of object B
id
hash The ID of object B
group
hash The collision group of object B

messages.name: trigger_event
messages.description: See physics.set_event_listener. This message is sent to a function specified in physics.set_event_listener when a collision object interacts with another collision object and one of them is a trigger. This message only reports that an interaction actually happened and will be sent once per colliding pair and frame. For more detailed information, check for the contact_point_event.

payload.name: enter
payload.doc: if the interaction was an entry or not

payload.name: a
payload.doc:
interaction information for object A
id
hash The ID of object A
group
hash The collision group of object A

payload.name: b
payload.doc:
collision information for object B
id
hash The ID of object B
group
hash The collision group of object B

messages.name: resize
messages.description: Set the size of the game window. Only works on desktop platforms.

payload.name: height
payload.doc: the new window height

payload.name: width
payload.doc: the new window width

messages.name: exit
messages.description: Terminates the game application and reports the specified code to the OS. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.

payload.name: code
payload.doc: exit code to report to the OS, 0 means clean exit

messages.name: toggle_profile
messages.description: Toggles the on-screen profiler. The profiler is a real-time tool that shows the numbers of milliseconds spent in each scope per frame as well as counters. The profiler is very useful for tracking down performance and resource problems. In addition to the on-screen profiler, Defold includes a web-based profiler that allows you to sample a series of data points and then analyze them in detail. The web profiler is available at http://<device IP>:8002 where is the IP address of the device you are running your game on. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.

messages.name: toggle_physics_debug
messages.description: Toggles the on-screen physics visual debugging mode which is very useful for tracking down issues related to physics. This mode visualizes all collision object shapes and normals at detected contact points. Toggling this mode on is equal to setting physics.debug in the "game.project" settings, but set in run-time. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.

messages.name: start_record
messages.description: Starts video recording of the game frame-buffer to file. Current video format is the open vp8 codec in the ivf container. It's possible to upload this format directly to YouTube. The VLC video player has native support but with the known issue that not the entire file is played back. It's probably an issue with VLC. The Miro Video Converter has support for vp8/ivf. Video recording is only supported on desktop platforms. Audio is currently not supported Window width and height must be a multiple of 8 to be able to record video. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.

payload.name: file_name
payload.doc: file name to write the video to

payload.name: frame_period
payload.doc: frame period to record, ie write every nth frame. Default value is 2

payload.name: fps
payload.doc: frames per second. Playback speed for the video. Default value is 30. The fps value doens't affect the recording. It's only meta-data in the written video file.

messages.name: stop_record
messages.description: Stops the currently active video recording. Video recording is only supported on desktop platforms. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.

messages.name: reboot
messages.description: Reboots the game engine with a specified set of arguments. Arguments will be translated into command line arguments. Sending the reboot command is equivalent to starting the engine with the same arguments. On startup the engine reads configuration from "game.project" in the project root. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.

payload.name: arg1
payload.doc: argument 1

payload.name: arg2
payload.doc: argument 2

payload.name: arg3
payload.doc: argument 3

payload.name: arg4
payload.doc: argument 4

payload.name: arg5
payload.doc: argument 5

payload.name: arg6
payload.doc: argument 6

messages.name: set_vsync
messages.description: Set the vsync swap interval. The interval with which to swap the front and back buffers in sync with vertical blanks (v-blank), the hardware event where the screen image is updated with data from the front buffer. A value of 1 swaps the buffers at every v-blank, a value of 2 swaps the buffers every other v-blank and so on. A value of 0 disables waiting for v-blank before swapping the buffers. Default value is 1. When setting the swap interval to 0 and having vsync disabled in "game.project", the engine will try to respect the set frame cap value from "game.project" in software instead. This setting may be overridden by driver settings. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.

payload.name: swap_interval
payload.doc: target swap interval.

messages.name: set_update_frequency
messages.description: Set game update-frequency (frame cap). This option is equivalent to display.update_frequency in the "game.project" settings but set in run-time. If Vsync checked in "game.project", the rate will be clamped to a swap interval that matches any detected main monitor refresh rate. If Vsync is unchecked the engine will try to respect the rate in software using timers. There is no guarantee that the frame cap will be achieved depending on platform specifics and hardware settings. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.

payload.name: frequency
payload.doc: target frequency. 60 for 60 fps

messages.name: resume_rendering
messages.description: Resume rendering. This message can only be sent to the designated @system socket.
